title: MetaBlooms Failure Pattern Card
description: Schema for a single failure pattern card in the MPP pattern library. Every field is required except countermeasures (minimum 1) and sources (minimum 1).

id.description: Stable, unique identifier in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE. Never reuse or rename after first commit.

name.description: Human-readable pattern name. Descriptive, not jargon.

class.description: The system layer where this pattern originates.

status.description: DRAFT = under review. ACTIVE = validated and committed. DEPRECATED = superseded.

tags.description: Search tags for retrieval. Include technology names, failure modes, and affected layers.

trigger_shapes.description: Code or architecture shapes that should trigger checking this pattern. Plain English. E.g. 'retry loop around an external API call'.

activation.description: The specific condition that starts the failure. What is the precipitating event?

mechanism.description: Step-by-step causal chain from activation to customer impact. Each step must follow from the prior one.

signal.description: What is visible (or invisible) at this phase. Specific enough to configure an alert on.

signal_sequence.description: Ordered phases of signal visibility from initial failure to full customer impact.

lag.description: Estimated time from activation to customer-visible impact. E.g. 'seconds', 'minutes', 'hours', 'days'.

threshold_estimated.description: The approximate load, rate, or condition at which this pattern typically activates. Include reasoning.

invisible_because.description: The specific reason standard monitoring and alerting does not catch this early. Why does it look OK when it isn't?

diagnostic.description: The concrete question or query an engineer runs to confirm or rule out this pattern. Specific enough to execute.

countermeasures.description: Concrete actions that prevent or mitigate this pattern. Each is actionable, not advisory.

sources.description: Evidence sources. At least one must be a postmortem or engineering-blog.

discovered.description: ISO 8601 date when this pattern was first documented in this library.

last_reviewed.description: ISO 8601 date of most recent review. Update whenever a source is added or a field is revised.
